findings.title: Editing a foreign-currency settlement (name-only companions) silently re-denominates it to the home currency, corrupting the per-currency Settle view
findings.rootCause: openEditSettlementModal's legacy-expense branch is hard-coded to the home currency: the amount input value is convertCurrency(s.euroValue, 'EUR', home) (line 462) and the submit handler sets `s.currency = home` and `s.euroValue = convertCurrency(amount, home, 'EUR')` (lines 486-487), with no currency selector. The original row's currency is discarded. computeTripBalancesByCurrency (balances.ts:253-269) keys strictly on exp.currency+value, so changing the currency moves the settlement to a different per-currency bucket and the original debt re-opens. The server-settlement edit path (openEditServerSettlementModal) correctly preserves `cur = s.currency` (line 534) — only the legacy expense path regresses.

findings.title: Budget stored in a non-EUR currency (via API/legacy) is read as EUR everywhere, producing wrong spent-vs-target status and Insights totals
findings.rootCause: budgets.py validates and persists `currency` + `amount` verbatim (lines 48-49, INSERT line 203) and there is NO normalization to EUR on the server. Every reader, however, assumes amount is EUR: budgetStatus uses `target = budget.amount` while `spent = Σ euroValue` (helpers.ts:196-199); Budgets.tsx renders `formatHome(b.amount, 'EUR')` (line 352); Insights does `convertCurrency(stat.target, 'EUR', targetCurr)` and `convertCurrency(stat.spent, 'EUR', targetCurr)` (Insights.tsx:480-481). budget.currency is read only for the cosmetic 'was X' suffix, never for the math. The expense write path keeps original currency + a real euro_value, but the budget path has no euro_value column and silently treats amount as EUR.

findings.title: Settling the exact foreign-currency debt clears the per-currency Settle view but leaves a residual in the EUR balance / Global tab / Insights (settle-time FX vs frozen expense FX)
findings.rootCause: The suggested debts come from computeTripBalancesByCurrency, which nets the expense's RAW `value` in its own currency (balances.ts:255) and subtracts the settlement's raw `amount` — so settling the exact foreign amount zeroes that bucket. But the EUR balance headline (SettlementView.tsx:175 → computeTripBalances) and the Global/Insights views net the expense's euro_value FROZEN at the expense's write-time rate (balances.ts:183), while the settlement contributes settlement.euroValue (balances.ts:92) computed at settle-time via convertCurrency(amount, currency, 'EUR') (actions.ts:115). When the FX rate moved between expense entry and settlement, frozen-euro_value(expense) != settle-time-euro_value(settlement), so the EUR net does not reach zero even though the foreign amounts match exactly. The two coexisting balance models (raw-currency vs frozen-EUR) disagree by the FX drift.

findings.title: convertCurrency silently falls back to 1:1 for any code missing from both live and static tables, so a stored amount in a now-unsupported currency renders at a fabricated rate
findings.rootCause: _rateFor returns `CONVERSION_RATES[upper] || 1` (currency.ts:77) — the 1.0 fallback is silent. convertCurrency multiplies by this 1:1 rate (currency.ts:119-123), and formatHome (currency.ts:137) and Insights/Settle display callers route through it without first checking hasRate(). The write paths were hardened (expenses C1, settlements S2, budgets IA-10 all gate on get_rate_eur / hasRate), but the DISPLAY-time convertCurrency keeps the legacy 1:1 fallback, so a row that slipped in under the old rules — or whose code later leaves the feed — renders at a fabricated rate. Balance/budget math reads the stored euro_value (safe), but the original-amount and per-currency display hints call convertCurrency directly.
